title: Henoch-Schönlein Purpura
chief_complaint: Rash on his legs and buttocks, swollen painful knees and ankles, and abdominal pain. He just got over a cold last week.

physical_exam.Skin: Palpable purpura on lower extremities and buttocks (non-thrombocytopenic - key feature). Lesions do NOT blanch. Some urticarial.
physical_exam.Joints: Swollen, tender knees and ankles bilaterally. No erythema. Range of motion limited by pain.
physical_exam.Abdomen: Diffuse colicky abdominal pain. No rebound. No guarding. Heme-occult positive stool (mild GI bleeding).
physical_exam.Genitalia: No scrotal swelling (rules out testicular torsion).

hidden_data.cbc: WBC 10,500, Hb 12.0, Platelets 380,000 (platelets NORMAL - key for diagnosis)
hidden_data.urinalysis: Microscopic hematuria (10-20 RBCs/hpf), mild proteinuria (1+). No RBC casts.
hidden_data.throat_culture: Group A Streptococcus positive (recent infection trigger)

correct_diagnosis: Henoch-Schönlein Purpura (HSP) - IgA Vasculitis with Skin, Joint, GI, and Mild Renal Involvement
key_learning_points: HSP = most common childhood vasculitis (peak age 4-7 years). IgA-mediated leukocytoclastic vasculitis of small vessels

Classic tetrad: Palpable purpura (LOWER EXTREMITIES + BUTTOCKS - gravity-dependent, pathognomonic) + Arthritis/arthralgia (knees/ankles, non-erosive, resolves) + Abdominal pain (colicky, may have GI bleeding, intussusception risk) + Renal disease (hematuria, proteinuria)
Diagnosis: CLINICAL. Normal platelets (differentiates from ITP/TTP). Normal coagulation (rules out DIC). Biopsy if atypical: IgA deposition on immunofluorescence
Treatment: SUPPORTIVE for most. NSAIDs for joint pain (ibuprofen). Prednisone 1-2mg/kg for severe abdominal pain, GI bleeding, or scrotal involvement. NO routine steroids for skin or joints alone

COMPLICATION: Intussusception (ileoileal, not ileocolic like idiopathic - ultrasound for diagnosis). Renal: 1-5% develop chronic GN. Monitor urinalysis and BP weekly for first month, then monthly for 6 months
